items.briefing:
Recent reports highlight how scammers are using increasingly sophisticated techniques to impersonate trusted organisations, with CommBank warning Australians to be extra vigilant. Separately, the Australian Financial Review has detailed how AI-powered fraud has been used to deceive even Australia's largest bank, demonstrating that these threats are evolving rapidly and can fool even well-resourced institutions. These stories are a reminder that scam attempts targeting our staff and students may appear more convincing than ever before.

On a positive note, Australian organisations are strengthening their cyber defences. Australia Post has announced a partnership to expand AI-driven cyber threat detection, while Macquarie Technology Group and Macquarie University are collaborating on cyber security and cloud research. These developments show the growing focus on protecting Australian institutions and may lead to improved security tools and practices across sectors, including education.

**Practical tip:** Before acting on any unexpected request for money, personal details, or login credentials—whether by email, phone, or message—always verify the request through official channels. Contact the organisation directly using a phone number or website you know is legitimate, not one provided in the suspicious message.
